role: Chairman of the Assembly of Experts for Leadership (1983–2007)
bio: Ayatollah Ali Meshkini was a senior cleric who served as the first and longest-serving chairman of the [Assembly of Experts](/orgs/assembly-of-experts) for Leadership from its establishment in 1983 until his death in 2007. He was elected to the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) in 1979, representing East Azerbaijan Province. A loyal supporter of [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) and later [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei), he was based in the Qom seminary and was a member of the [Society of Seminary Teachers of Qom](/orgs/society-of-seminary-teachers-of-qom). His long chairmanship of the Assembly of Experts gave him a pivotal role in the supervision of the Supreme Leader's office, though the assembly rarely exercised its oversight powers during his tenure.
editorial_notes: Born 1921 in Meshkinshahr. Died 1 August 2007 in Qom. Born Ali-Akbar Feyz-Alani. Chairman of Assembly of Experts for Leadership 1983–2007 (24 years).
